summary: Matched 14 functions and improved another 15 in the trophy (`ty`) module, mainly across `toy.c`, `tylist.c`, and `tyfigupon.c`. The PR added trophy data/types, unlock-state logic, list/table traversal, language-dependent trophy metadata lookup, fog/light/display helpers, and dev-text trophy UI routines. It also updated callers and headers for the changed `un_803063D4` signature and moved a Kirby extern declaration into the static header. Review focused on keeping newly known structs instead of reverting to raw `M2C_FIELD`, eliminating unnecessary casts and brittle pointer math, extracting structs where possible, justifying new struct fields, and using inlined string accesses for matching.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed trophy/toy functions in `src/melee/ty/toy.c`, including `un_803053C4`, `Trophy_SetUnlockState`, `un_803060BC`, `un_803062BC`, `un_80306930`, `un_80306A0C`, `un_80306C5C`, `un_80307F64`, `un_803082F8`, `un_80308328`, `un_80308354`, `un_803109A0`, `un_803114E8`, and `un_80311788` as shown in the diff excerpt.
Added trophy data lookup behavior in `un_803060BC`, selecting JP or US trophy table values depending on language settings and returning fields by numeric field index.

Added trophy unlock/update logic in `Trophy_SetUnlockState`, including choosing between the local `Toy` save-like table and `gmMainLib_8015CC78()` depending on game mode, toggling high-bit state, incrementing trophy count, clamping the low byte to `0xFF`, triggering completion behavior at count `0xFA`, and invoking related GM/trophy side effects.

Implemented list/archive setup and trophy-list handling in `tylist.c`; excerpts show archive symbol loading via `lbArchive_LoadSymbols`, `gmMainLib_8015CC78`, `gmMainLib_8015CC84`, language checks, and loops over `0x125` trophy entries.

Expanded trophy-related type information in `src/melee/ty/types.h`, including `ToyAnimState`, `ToyEntry`, `TrophyData`, and additional `Toy` fields such as `x19C`, `trophyTable[0x125]`, and `trophyCount` according to review excerpts.
Updated `ifprize.c` caller from `un_803063D4()` to `un_803063D4(0, 0, 0)` to match the new signature.
Moved `ftKb_Init_804D9570` extern from `ftKb_Init.c` into `ftKb_Init.static.h`, with only formatting/no-behavior change to the `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` call shown.
Adjusted trophy headers (`toy.h`, `tylist.h`, `tyfigupon.h`, `tydisplay.h`) to expose the new or corrected signatures/types needed by the matched code.
smart_moves: Converted some raw memory access into local structs such as `TyViewData`, `TyDisplayData`, `TyLightData`, `ToyAnimState`, `ToyEntry`, and `TrophyData`, making offsets searchable and reusable for later `ty` decomp work.
Kept trophy table traversal explicit around the known trophy count `0x125`, which documents the table size used by unlock/list logic.
Modeled trophy table entries with a sentinel `id == -1` in `TrophyData` loops, matching the data-driven lookup pattern visible in `un_803060BC` and `un_80308354`.
Updated the cross-file caller in `ifprize.c` immediately after changing `un_803063D4`'s signature, avoiding stale prototype/callsite drift.
Handled JP/US trophy table selection directly in lookup code, preserving language-dependent behavior instead of flattening the data tables.
Centralized the Kirby float data extern in the static header rather than leaving a function-local extern in `ftKb_Init.c`.
decomp_lessons: When matching trophy/list code, prefer extracting structs for repeated offset access instead of relying on `M2C_FIELD`; review explicitly called out reverting away from `TyModeState` as undesirable.
Pointer math in decompiled output should be revisited once table element types are known. Review requested fixing all pointer math in `tylist.c`, especially around table/list walks.
Do not keep casts just because they help an early match if the called function can be typed correctly; review questioned casts on `gmMainLib_8015CC78()` and `gmMainLib_8015CC84()` results.

New struct fields in shared headers need justification from layout, table size, access patterns, or symbol usage. Review challenged confidence for `ToyAnimState`, `TrophyData`, `trophyTable`, and `trophyCount`.
String access style can affect matching. PsiLupan requested replacing string pointer-offset accesses with the inlined string form in `tylist.c`.
For language-dependent assets, preserve both saved-language and current-setting checks; the trophy module compares JP/US setting combinations before selecting table values.
When a signature changes in a partially decompiled module, audit and update all callers, even outside the main system being worked on.
naming_conventions: Matched but still-unknown functions retain `un_80xxxxxx` names, e.g. `un_803053C4`, `un_803060BC`, `un_803063D4`, `un_803109A0`.
A semantically understood function was named `Trophy_SetUnlockState`, indicating trophy-specific behavior can justify promotion out of `un_` naming.
New placeholder struct fields use offset-style names such as `x0E`, `x19C`, `x20`, and `pad_18`, preserving layout while avoiding invented semantics.
Local temporary structs use module/system prefixes, e.g. `TyViewData`, `TyDisplayData`, `TyLightData`, `ToyAnimState`, `ToyEntry`, and `TrophyData`.
Header/static declaration pattern places file-local external data such as `ftKb_Init_804D9570` in `ftKb_Init.static.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit `goto` labels such as `check_found`, `check_found2`, `done`, and `loop` to preserve control-flow shape in trophy loops.

Used byte-offset access through `(u8*) table + byteOffset` for trophy unlock state entries when the match required byte-level address computation, though review requested cleaning pointer math where possible.
Copied a small `ToyEntry` table from `un_803B8910` onto the stack in `un_803109A0`, including pointer-walk and unrolled comparisons to reproduce generated code shape.
Added a dummy stack array in `un_803114E8` (`s32 pad[0x20]`) to force a larger stack frame, a common matching tactic when stack layout matters.
Used `M2C_FIELD` only where types were still unresolved, but review feedback indicates future work should replace it with known structs when possible.
Preserved explicit casts to function pointer types for `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` callbacks where required by prototype shape.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime

review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether casts around `gmMainLib_8015CC78()` / `gmMainLib_8015CC84()` were necessary.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether an accessed object had a known struct and whether one could be extracted.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked for justification/confidence for newly added trophy/toy structs and fields.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Requested string accesses be fixed to use the inlined string form.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `TrophyData`, `Toy`, `trophyTable`, or `trophyCount`.
Check whether `un_803063D4` was fully matched and named after this signature change.
Search for follow-up cleanup replacing `M2C_FIELD` in `tydisplay.c`, `toy.c`, and `tylist.c` with `TyModeState` or other structs.
Look for later PRs addressing PsiLupan's inlined string access feedback in `tylist.c`.
Audit trophy table size `0x125` and sentinel `-1` assumptions against later type definitions.
Find whether `un_803060BC` field indices 0 through 8 were later converted to an enum.
